2024-01-25 6 min read by Matthew Brunken

Setting and Achieving Your Running Goals

Learn how to set SMART goals for your running journey and stay motivated throughout your fitness transformation.

Setting goals is crucial for long-term running success. Whether you're aiming to complete your first 5K or qualify for Boston, having clear, well-defined goals can keep you motivated and on track. Here's how to set goals that work.

The SMART Framework

Apply the SMART criteria to your running goals:

  • Specific: Instead of "I want to run more," say "I want to run 20 miles per week."
  • Measurable: How will you track progress? Mileage, pace, heart rate?
  • Achievable: Is this goal realistic given your current fitness and schedule?
  • Relevant: Does this goal align with your broader fitness aspirations?
  • Time-bound: When do you want to achieve this by?

Short-Term vs. Long-Term Goals

Balance short-term goals (this week, this month) with long-term goals (this year, 5 years). Short-term goals keep you engaged day-to-day, while long-term goals provide direction and motivation for the bigger picture.

Process Goals vs. Outcome Goals

Focus on process goals - things you can control like consistent training, strength work, and proper recovery. While outcome goals (finishing a race, hitting a specific pace) are important, excessive focus on outcomes you can't control can lead to frustration.

Tracking Your Progress

Keep a training log to track your runs, how you felt, and your achievements. Many runners find apps and GPS watches helpful for monitoring progress over time. Celebrate milestones along the way!

Dealing with setbacks

Injury, illness, and life circumstances will occasionally derail your training. This is normal and expected. The key is to adjust your goals as needed, focus on what you can do, and avoid being too hard on yourself.

Accountability

Share your goals with friends, family, or a running community. Having others aware of your aspirations can provide extra motivation and support when you need it most.
